constructors.name: Projection
constructors.summary: Constructs a default-initialized [Projection] identical to [constant IDENTITY]. [b]Note:[/b] In C#, this constructs a [Projection] identical to [constant ZERO].

constructors.name: Projection
constructors.summary: Constructs a [Projection] as a copy of the given [Projection].

constructors.name: Projection
constructors.summary: Constructs a Projection as a copy of the given [Transform3D].

constructors.name: Projection
constructors.signature: public Projection(Vector4 xAxis, Vector4 yAxis, Vector4 zAxis, Vector4 wAxis)
constructors.summary: Constructs a Projection from four [Vector4] values (matrix columns).

members.name: CreateDepthCorrection
members.summary: Creates a new [Projection] that projects positions from a depth range of [code]-1[/code] to [code]1[/code] to one that ranges from [code]0[/code] to [code]1[/code], and flips the projected positions vertically, according to [param flip_y].

members.name: CreateFitAabb
members.summary: Creates a new [Projection] that scales a given projection to fit around a given [AABB] in projection space.

members.name: CreateForHmd
members.signature: public Projection CreateForHmd(int eye, float aspect, float intraocularDist, float displayWidth, float displayToLens, float oversample, float zNear, float zFar)
members.summary: Creates a new [Projection] for projecting positions onto a head-mounted display with the given X:Y aspect ratio, distance between eyes, display width, distance to lens, oversampling factor, and depth clipping planes. [param eye] creates the projection for the left eye when set to 1, or the right eye when set to 2.

members.name: CreateFrustum
members.signature: public Projection CreateFrustum(float left, float right, float bottom, float top, float zNear, float zFar)
members.summary: Creates a new [Projection] that projects positions in a frustum with the given clipping planes.

members.name: CreateFrustumAspect
members.signature: public Projection CreateFrustumAspect(float size, float aspect, Vector2 offset, float zNear, float zFar, bool flipFov)
members.summary: Creates a new [Projection] that projects positions in a frustum with the given size, X:Y aspect ratio, offset, and clipping planes. [param flip_fov] determines whether the projection's field of view is flipped over its diagonal.

members.name: CreateLightAtlasRect
members.summary: Creates a new [Projection] that projects positions into the given [Rect2].

members.name: CreateOrthogonal
members.signature: public Projection CreateOrthogonal(float left, float right, float bottom, float top, float zNear, float zFar)
members.summary: Creates a new [Projection] that projects positions using an orthogonal projection with the given clipping planes.

members.name: CreateOrthogonalAspect
members.signature: public Projection CreateOrthogonalAspect(float size, float aspect, float zNear, float zFar, bool flipFov)
members.summary: Creates a new [Projection] that projects positions using an orthogonal projection with the given size, X:Y aspect ratio, and clipping planes. [param flip_fov] determines whether the projection's field of view is flipped over its diagonal.

members.name: CreatePerspective
members.signature: public Projection CreatePerspective(float fovy, float aspect, float zNear, float zFar, bool flipFov)
members.summary: Creates a new [Projection] that projects positions using a perspective projection with the given Y-axis field of view (in degrees), X:Y aspect ratio, and clipping planes. [param flip_fov] determines whether the projection's field of view is flipped over its diagonal.

members.name: CreatePerspectiveHmd
members.signature: public Projection CreatePerspectiveHmd(float fovy, float aspect, float zNear, float zFar, bool flipFov, int eye, float intraocularDist, float convergenceDist)
members.summary: Creates a new [Projection] that projects positions using a perspective projection with the given Y-axis field of view (in degrees), X:Y aspect ratio, and clipping distances. The projection is adjusted for a head-mounted display with the given distance between eyes and distance to a point that can be focused on. [param eye] creates the projection for the left eye when set to 1, or the right eye when set to 2. [param flip_fov] determines whether the projection's field of view is flipped over its diagonal.

members.name: Determinant
members.summary: Returns a scalar value that is the signed factor by which areas are scaled by this matrix. If the sign is negative, the matrix flips the orientation of the area. The determinant can be used to calculate the invertibility of a matrix or solve linear systems of equations involving the matrix, among other applications.

members.name: FlippedY
members.summary: Returns a copy of this [Projection] with the signs of the values of the Y column flipped.

members.name: GetAspect
members.summary: Returns the X:Y aspect ratio of this [Projection]'s viewport.

members.name: GetFarPlaneHalfExtents
members.summary: Returns the dimensions of the far clipping plane of the projection, divided by two.

members.name: GetFov
members.summary: Returns the horizontal field of view of the projection (in degrees).

members.name: GetFovy
members.signature: public float GetFovy(float fovx, float aspect)
members.summary: Returns the vertical field of view of the projection (in degrees) associated with the given horizontal field of view (in degrees) and aspect ratio. [b]Note:[/b] Unlike most methods of [Projection], [param aspect] is expected to be 1 divided by the X:Y aspect ratio.

members.name: GetLodMultiplier
members.summary: Returns the factor by which the visible level of detail is scaled by this [Projection].

members.name: GetPixelsPerMeter
members.summary: Returns [param for_pixel_width] divided by the viewport's width measured in meters on the near plane, after this [Projection] is applied.

members.name: GetProjectionPlane
members.summary: Returns the clipping plane of this [Projection] whose index is given by [param plane]. [param plane] should be equal to one of [constant PLANE_NEAR], [constant PLANE_FAR], [constant PLANE_LEFT], [constant PLANE_TOP], [constant PLANE_RIGHT], or [constant PLANE_BOTTOM].

members.name: GetViewportHalfExtents
members.summary: Returns the dimensions of the viewport plane that this [Projection] projects positions onto, divided by two.

members.name: GetZFar
members.summary: Returns the distance for this [Projection] beyond which positions are clipped.

members.name: GetZNear
members.summary: Returns the distance for this [Projection] before which positions are clipped.

members.name: Inverse
members.summary: Returns a [Projection] that performs the inverse of this [Projection]'s projective transformation.

members.name: IsOrthogonal
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if this [Projection] performs an orthogonal projection.

members.name: JitterOffseted
members.summary: Returns a [Projection] with the X and Y values from the given [Vector2] added to the first and second values of the final column respectively.

members.name: PerspectiveZnearAdjusted
members.summary: Returns a [Projection] with the near clipping distance adjusted to be [param new_znear]. [b]Note:[/b] The original [Projection] must be a perspective projection.

members.name: W
members.summary: The projection matrix's W vector (column 3). Equivalent to array index [code]3[/code].

members.name: X
members.summary: The projection matrix's X vector (column 0). Equivalent to array index [code]0[/code].

members.name: Y
members.summary: The projection matrix's Y vector (column 1). Equivalent to array index [code]1[/code].

members.name: Z
members.summary: The projection matrix's Z vector (column 2). Equivalent to array index [code]2[/code].

values.name: PLANE_BOTTOM
values.summary: The index value of the projection bottom clipping plane.

values.name: PLANE_FAR
values.summary: The index value of the projection's far clipping plane.

values.name: PLANE_LEFT
values.summary: The index value of the projection's left clipping plane.

values.name: PLANE_NEAR
values.summary: The index value of the projection's near clipping plane.

values.name: PLANE_RIGHT
values.summary: The index value of the projection's right clipping plane.

values.name: PLANE_TOP
values.summary: The index value of the projection's top clipping plane.

constants.name: PLANE_NEAR
constants.summary: The index value of the projection's near clipping plane.

constants.name: PLANE_FAR
constants.summary: The index value of the projection's far clipping plane.

constants.name: PLANE_LEFT
constants.summary: The index value of the projection's left clipping plane.

constants.name: PLANE_TOP
constants.summary: The index value of the projection's top clipping plane.

constants.name: PLANE_RIGHT
constants.summary: The index value of the projection's right clipping plane.

constants.name: PLANE_BOTTOM
constants.summary: The index value of the projection bottom clipping plane.

constants.name: IDENTITY
constants.summary: A [Projection] with no transformation defined. When applied to other data structures, no transformation is performed.

constants.name: ZERO
constants.summary: A [Projection] with all values initialized to 0. When applied to other data structures, they will be zeroed.

operators.name: operator !=
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the projections are not equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, this may return [code]true[/code], even if the projections are virtually equal. An [code]is_equal_approx[/code] method may be added in a future version of Godot.

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Returns a [Projection] that applies the combined transformations of this [Projection] and [param right].

operators.name: operator *
operators.summary: Projects (multiplies) the given [Vector4] by this [Projection] matrix.

operators.name: operator ==
operators.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if the projections are equal. [b]Note:[/b] Due to floating-point precision errors, this may return [code]false[/code], even if the projections are virtually equal. An [code]is_equal_approx[/code] method may be added in a future version of Godot.

operators.name: operator []
operators.summary: Returns the column of the [Projection] with the given index. Indices are in the following order: x, y, z, w.
